description: Currently 'ao daemon stop' kills the daemon process immediately, leaving in-flight agent executions orphaned. Implement graceful shutdown: (1) stop the scheduler from spawning new agents, (2) send SIGTERM to the daemon which enters drain mode, (3) wait up to --shutdown-timeout-secs (default 60) for running agents to complete, (4) if timeout exceeded, cancel remaining workflows cleanly, (5) only then exit. Also make daemon pause properly checkpoint workflow state before suspending scheduler ticks.
title: Make daemon stop gracefully wait for in-flight agents before exit
